channel.title: 3. Blogs Posted :: Internship Report - Phan Thi Khanh Bang
channel.description: Welcome to the Blogs Posted section! During my learning and internship journey at AWS, I regularly read deep-dive articles on the AWS Blog. Below is a summary of the 3 most outstanding articles that I have carefully studied and drawn many practical lessons from. Click on the titles to view my full detailed reflections: 1. Building Highly Available Oracle Databases with Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP This blog provides a practical perspective on how to combine multiple services (Amazon FSx, EC2 Auto Scaling, AWS Backup, Lambda, and Systems Manager) to create a self-healing architecture. My biggest takeaway is not just how to deploy a database, but the mindset of designing a highly available system that ensures the minimum possible Recovery Time Objective (RTO) and Recovery Point Objective (RPO).

item.title: 3.1. Build your first search application with Amazon OpenSearch Service
item.description: In the era of data explosion, the ability to search and analyze information in real-time is no longer just an advantage but a vital condition for businesses. From industrial IoT sensors streaming millions of metrics per second, to e-commerce platforms needing to display products instantly, or security teams needing to detect threats in real-time—all require a powerful search platform. Amazon OpenSearch Service was created to solve exactly this problem. What is OpenSearch Service? This is a fully managed search and analytics service by AWS. Instead of worrying about infrastructure, you just focus on building your application. The Amazon OpenSearch Serverless version goes even further by automatically scaling resources according to demand without managing servers.

item.title: 3.2. How are AI and Robotics transforming sustainable agriculture with Amazon SageMaker AI?
item.description: Today I want to share a very interesting article from the AWS Architecture Blog about how Aigen - an autonomous agricultural robotics company - modernized its entire AI pipeline using Amazon SageMaker AI to build a smart and sustainable farming system. In modern agriculture, using robots to identify weeds, monitor crops, or optimize yield is no longer unfamiliar. However, as the number of robots increases, new challenges emerge: Robots operate in areas with unstable Internet connections. Thousands of images are collected daily that need labeling to train AI. On-premises GPU infrastructure lacks the capacity to handle simultaneous model training and data labeling. Slow model update cycles make it difficult for robots to adapt to real-world field conditions. How to scale to hundreds of robots in the field without continuously investing in more GPU servers? AWS and Aigen solved this problem with a cloud-native AI architecture.

item.title: 3.3. Automate medical record digitization with Amazon Bedrock Data Automation and AWS HealthLake
item.description: Today I want to share a very interesting architecture from AWS for the healthcare sector: automating the digitization of medical records using AI with Amazon Bedrock Data Automation combined with AWS HealthLake. In the digital transformation process, many hospitals still store medical records in paper or scanned PDF formats. This makes searching for information, aggregating treatment histories, or sharing data across systems very time-consuming. Manual data entry is not only resource-intensive but also prone to errors.
